A1.label: British customs officer Ebenezer Richardson fires blindly into a crowd during a protest in North End, Boston, fatally wounding 11-year-old Christopher Seider; the first American fatality of the American Revolution.

A2.label: The Franciscan Girolamo Maschi is elected pope, choosing the name Nicholas IV.

A3.label: President Grover Cleveland signs a bill admitting North Dakota, South Dakota, Montana and Washington as U.S. states.

A4.label: Filipino forces led by General Antonio Luna launch counterattacks for the first time against the American forces during the Philippine–American War. The Filipinos fail to regain Manila from the Americans.

A5.label: The United Kingdom sells a meteorological station on the South Orkney Islands to Argentina; the islands are subsequently claimed by the United Kingdom in 1908.

A6.label: Saddam Hussein begins the Anfal genocide against Kurds and Assyrians in northern Iraq.

A7.label: Iran hostage crisis: Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini states that Iran's parliament will decide the fate of the American embassy hostages.

A8.label: Berbice slave uprising in Guyana: The first major slave revolt in South America.

A9.label: J. S. Bach leads his Tafel-Music Shepherd Cantata for the birthday of Christian, Duke of Saxe-Weissenfels.

A10.label: American Revolutionary War: Baron von Steuben arrives at Valley Forge, Pennsylvania, to help train the Continental Army.

A11.label: The French Riviera is hit by a large earthquake, killing around 2,000.

A12.label: L'Orfeo by Claudio Monteverdi, one of the first works recognized as an opera, receives its première performance.

A13.label: The last battle of the Cudgel War takes place on the Santavuori Hill in Ilmajoki, Ostrobothnia.

A14.label: Nancy Astor becomes the first woman to speak in the House of Commons of the United Kingdom following her election as a Member of Parliament (MP) three months earlier.

A15.label: The Governor-General of Korea establishes a clinic called Jahyewon in Sorokdo to segregate Hansen's disease patients.

A17.label: Seven hundred ninety-one Romanian Jewish refugees and crew members are killed after the MV Struma is torpedoed by the Soviet Navy.

A18.label: Disbandment of the Warsaw Pact at a meeting of its members in Budapest.

A19.label: Marie-Adélaïde, the eldest of six daughters of Guillaume IV, becomes the first reigning Grand Duchess of Luxembourg.

A20.label: American-Israeli extremist Baruch Goldstein commits a mass shooting at the Cave of the Patriarchs mausoleum, leaving 29 dead and over 100 injured before he is disarmed and beaten to death by survivors.

A21.label: Hiram Rhodes Revels, a Republican from Mississippi, is sworn into the United States Senate, becoming the first African American ever to sit in Congress.

A22.label: Alitalia Flight 1553 crashes at Genoa Cristoforo Colombo Airport in Genoa, Italy, killing four.

A23.label: Soldiers of the Bangladesh Rifles mutiny at their headquarters in Pilkhana, Dhaka, Bangladesh, resulting in 74 deaths, including 57 army officials.

A24.label: The New York Philharmonic performs in Pyongyang, North Korea; this is the first event of its kind to take place in North Korea.

A25.label: HMHS Britannic, sister to the RMS Titanic, is launched at Harland and Wolff shipyard in Belfast.

A26.label: In the February 26 Incident, young nationalist Japanese military officers assassinate multiple cabinet statesmen and start a rebellion in downtown Tokyo, which is ended 3 days later.

A27.label: The Beach Pneumatic Transit in New York City, intended as a demonstration for a subway line, opens.

A28.label: Japan and Korea sign the Treaty of Kangwha, which grants Japanese citizens extraterritoriality rights in Korea, opens three Korean ports to Japanese trade, and ends Korea's status as a tributary state of Qing dynasty China.

A29.label: The Janszoon voyage of 1605–06 becomes the first European expedition to set foot on Australia, although it is mistaken as a part of New Guinea.

A30.label: Adolf Hitler orders the Luftwaffe to be re-formed, violating the provisions of the Treaty of Versailles.

A31.label: World War II: US troops reclaim the Philippine island of Corregidor from the Japanese.

A32.label: A total of 279 female students aged between 10 and 17 are kidnapped by bandits in the Zamfara kidnapping in Zamfara State, Nigeria.

A33.label: Indian Air Force fighter-jets target Jaish-e-Mohammed terrorist training camps in Balakot, Pakistan.

A34.label: A New York-bound Alitalia airliner crashes into a cemetery in Shannon, Ireland, shortly after takeoff, killing 34 of the 52 persons on board.

A35.label: Abaoji, chieftain of the Yila tribe, is named khagan of the Khitans.

A36.label: Sumgait pogrom: The Armenian community in Sumgait, Azerbaijan is targeted in a violent pogrom.

A37.label: The Holocaust: In Berlin, the Gestapo arrest 1,800 Jewish men with German wives, leading to the Rosenstrasse protest.

A38.label: World War II: During the Battle of the Java Sea, an Allied strike force is defeated by a Japanese task force in the Java Sea in the Dutch East Indies.

A39.label: Martin Kamen and Sam Ruben discover carbon-14.

A40.label: The final episode of M*A*S*H airs, with almost 110 million viewers.

A41.label: A Turkish military memorandum results in the collapse of the coalition government in Turkey.

A42.label: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-36.

A43.label: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco and Firearms (ATF) agents raid the Branch Davidian church in Waco, Texas with a warrant to arrest the group's leader David Koresh, starting a 51-day standoff.

A44.label: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.

A45.label: Cold War: Klaus Fuchs is convicted of spying for the Soviet Union by disclosing top secret atomic bomb data.

A46.label: Bosnia and Herzegovina declares its independence from Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

A47.label: Battle of Adwa: An Ethiopian army defeats an outnumbered Italian force, ending the First Italo-Ethiopian War.

A49.label: Marshal F. S. López dies during the Battle of Cerro Corá thus marking the end of the Paraguayan War.

A50.label: In Roper v. Simmons, the U.S. Supreme Court rules that the execution of juveniles found guilty of any crime is unconstitutional.

A51.label: In Burma, the army led by General Ne Win seizes power in a coup d'état.

A52.label: Siege of Rome: The Ostrogoth army under king Vitiges begins the siege of the capital. Belisarius conducts a delaying action outside the Flaminian Gate; he and a detachment of his bucellarii are almost cut off.

A53.label: Texas Revolution: The Declaration of independence of the Republic of Texas from Mexico is adopted.

A54.label: Signing of the Kandyan Convention treaty by British invaders and the leaders of the Kingdom of Kandy.

A55.label: The Steel Workers Organizing Committee signs a collective bargaining agreement with U.S. Steel, leading to unionization of the United States steel industry.

A56.label: The College of Arms is formally incorporated by Royal Charter signed by King Richard III of England.

A57.label: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches from the Kennedy Space Center on STS-67, carrying the ASTRO-2 spacelab observatory.

A58.label: American Revolutionary War: The Continental Army is routed at the Battle of Brier Creek near Savannah, Georgia.

A59.label: US magazine Time publishes its first issue.

A60.label: The Ottoman Caliphate is abolished, when the Caliph Abdülmecid II of the Ottoman dynasty is deposed. The last remnant of the old monarchy gives way to the reformed Turkey of Kemal Atatürk.

A61.label: Oil is discovered in Saudi Arabia.

A62.label: A bomb blast in Karachi, Pakistan, kills at least 48 people and injured 200 others in a predominantly Shia Muslim area.

A63.label: Mughal Emperor Akbar defeats Sultan of Bengal Daud Khan Karrani's army at the Battle of Tukaroi.

A64.label: Nero, later to become Roman emperor, is given the title princeps iuventutis (head of the youth).

A65.label: Cyril VI of Constantinople is elected Ecumenical Patriarch of Constantinople.

A66.label: William McKinley inaugurated president for second time; Theodore Roosevelt is vice president.

A67.label: The Collinwood school fire, Collinwood near Cleveland, Ohio, kills 174 people.

A68.label: The first national flag of the Confederate States of America (the "Stars and Bars") is adopted.

A69.label: Field Marshal C. G. E. Mannerheim, the 6th president of Finland, resigns from his position for health reasons.

A70.label: Vermont is admitted to the United States as the fourteenth state.

A71.label: World War II: Japanese forces capture Batavia, capital of Dutch East Indies, which is left undefended after the withdrawal of the KNIL garrison and Australian Blackforce battalion to Buitenzorg and Bandung.

A72.label: An Antonov An-148 crashes in Russia's Alexeyevsky District, Belgorod Oblast during a test flight, killing all seven aboard.

A73.label: Air France Flight 212 crashes into La Grande Soufrière, killing all 63 aboard.

A74.label: Aeroflot Flight 191 crashes while landing at Aşgabat International Airport, killing 12.

A75.label: Mefistofele, an opera by Arrigo Boito, receives its premiere performance at La Scala.

A76.label: King Henry VII of England issues letters patent to John Cabot and his sons, authorising them to explore unknown lands.

A77.label: Nicolaus Copernicus's book On the Revolutions of the Heavenly Spheres is added to the Index of Forbidden Books 73 years after it was first published.

A78.label: An anarchist assassin tries to kill German Emperor Wilhelm II.

A79.label: World War II: Soviet Air Forces bomb the evacuated town of Narva in German-occupied Estonia, destroying the entire historical Swedish-era town.

A80.label: Treaty of Paris of 1323 is signed.

A81.label: Georgy Malenkov succeeds Joseph Stalin as Premier of the Soviet Union and First Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union.

A82.label: Ghana becomes the first Sub-Saharan country to gain independence from the British.

A83.label: The British ferry MS Herald of Free Enterprise capsizes in about 90 seconds, killing 193.

A84.label: International Unemployment Day demonstrations globally initiated by the Comintern.

A85.label: Konrad III von Hohenstaufen is elected king of Germany at Coblenz in the presence of the papal legate Theodwin.

A86.label: The short-lived socialist Labin Republic is proclaimed.

A87.label: Alexander Graham Bell is granted a patent for an invention he calls the "telephone".

A88.label: Shrigley abduction: 15-year old Ellen Turner is abducted by Edward Gibbon Wakefield, a future figure in the establishment of colonies in South Australia and New Zealand.

A89.label: Senator Daniel Webster gives his "Seventh of March" speech endorsing the Compromise of 1850 in order to prevent a possible civil war.

A90.label: Emperor Napoleon I of France wins the Battle of Craonne.

A91.label: French aviatrix Raymonde de Laroche becomes the first woman to receive a pilot's license.

A92.label: Twenty-eight political institutions in Myanmar establish the National Unity Consultative Council, a historic alliance of ethnic armed organizations and democratically elected leaders, in response to the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.

A93.label: Philips demonstrates the compact disc publicly for the first time.

A94.label: Ferdowsi completes his epic poem Shahnameh.

A95.label: Following the death of his mother, queen Urraca of León, Alfonso VII is proclaimed king of León.

A96.label: World War I: A British force unsuccessfully attempts to relieve the siege of Kut (present-day Iraq) in the Battle of Dujaila.

A97.label: International Women's Day protests in Petrograd mark the beginning of the February Revolution (February 23 in the Julian calendar).

A98.label: After a campaign by the writer Voltaire, judges in Paris posthumously exonerate Jean Calas of murdering his son. Calas had been tortured and executed in 1762 on the charge, though his son may have actually died by suicide.

A99.label: McCarthyism: CBS television broadcasts the See It Now episode, "A Report on Senator Joseph McCarthy", produced by Fred Friendly.

A100.label: The fleet of Pedro Álvares Cabral leaves Lisbon for the Indies. The fleet will discover Brazil which lies within boundaries granted to Portugal in the Treaty of Tordesillas in 1494.

A101.label: Safavid troops retreat from Basra, ending a three-year occupation.

A102.label: Scottish philosopher Adam Smith publishes The Wealth of Nations, ushering in the classical period of political economy.

A103.label: Khwarazmian sultan Jalal ad-Din conquers the Georgian capital of Tbilisi.

A104.label: The Hanafi Siege: In a 39-hour standoff, armed Hanafi Muslims seize three Washington, D.C., buildings.

A105.label: The Dot-com bubble peaks with the NASDAQ Composite stock market index reaching 5,048.62.

A106.label: Roman Emperor Maximian concludes his campaign in North Africa and makes a triumphal entry into Carthage.

A107.label: Spaniard Fray Tomás de Berlanga, the fourth Bishop of Panama, discovers the Galápagos Islands by chance on his way to Peru.

A108.label: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302, a Boeing 737 MAX, crashes shortly after take off, killing all 157 passengers and crew. This and the prior Lion Air Flight 610 led to all 387 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft being grounded worldwide.

A109.label: After establishing the city of Santo Domingo, Christopher Columbus departs for Spain, leaving his brother in command.

A110.label: John Gorton resigns as Prime Minister of Australia and the leader of the Liberal Party of Australia after a secret ballot vote of confidence, being replaced in both positions by William McMahon.

A111.label: In Memphis, Tennessee, James Earl Ray pleads guilty to assassinating Martin Luther King Jr. He later unsuccessfully attempts to recant.

A112.label: In New York City, Samuel Roxy Rothafel opens the Roxy Theatre.

A113.label: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-123, carrying the first component of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.

A114.label: Economist and businessman Sebastián Piñera is sworn in as President of Chile. Aftershocks of the 2010 Pichilemu earthquakes hit central Chile during the ceremony.

A115.label: Lithuania declares independence from the Soviet Union.

A116.label: Mikhail Gorbachev is elected to the position of General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, making Gorbachev the USSR's de facto, and last, head of state.

A117.label: Battle of Castagnaro: Padua, led by John Hawkwood, is victorious over Giovanni Ordelaffi of Verona.

A118.label: The 1971 Turkish military memorandum is sent to the Süleyman Demirel government of Turkey and the government resigns.

A119.label: Vitiges, king of the Ostrogoths, ends his siege of Rome and retreats to Ravenna, leaving the city to the victorious Byzantine general, Belisarius.

A120.label: The World Health Organization officially release a global warning of outbreaks of Severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS).

A121.label: The future capital of Australia is officially named Canberra.

A122.label: Tim Berners-Lee submits his proposal to CERN for an information management system, which subsequently develops into the World Wide Web.

A123.label: Konrad von Wallenrode is elected the 24th Grand Master of the Teutonic Order (date is O.S.).

A124.label: Start of the Siege of Maastricht, part of the Eighty Years' War.

A125.label: British forces occupy Bloemfontein, Orange Free State, during the Second Boer War.

A126.label: The Battle of Cartagena de Indias (part of the War of Jenkins' Ear) begins.

A127.label: The Byzantine Empire and the Republic of Genoa sign a permanent treaty against the Venetians at Nymphaeum.

A128.label: The news of the discovery of Pluto is announced by Lowell Observatory.

A129.label: The Battle of Oosterweel, traditionally regarded as the start of the Eighty Years' War.

A130.label: The Ankara bombing kills at least 37 people.

A131.label: Breonna Taylor is killed by police officers who were forcibly entering her home in Louisville, Kentucky; her death sparked extensive protests against racism and police brutality.

A132.label: The Third Anglo-Dutch War: The Battle of Ronas Voe results in the Dutch East India Company ship Wapen van Rotterdam being captured with a death toll of up to 300 Dutch crew and soldiers.

A133.label: Thirty Years' War: Bavaria, Cologne, France and Sweden sign the Truce of Ulm.

A134.label: Jack Ruby is convicted of killing Lee Harvey Oswald, the assassin who had shot and killed John F. Kennedy the previous year.

A135.label: Battle of Ivry: Henry of Navarre and the Huguenots defeat the forces of the Catholic League under Charles, Duke of Mayenne, during the French Wars of Religion.

A136.label: Battle of Mogyoród: Dukes Géza and Ladislaus defeat their cousin Solomon, King of Hungary, forcing him to flee to Hungary's western borderland.
